visual_features.overall_style: Minimalist, editorial, high‑end beauty/ boutique aesthetic. Large amounts of white space, fine serif typography, and a single muted blush accent color create an elegant, calm, premium feel.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven almost entirely by typography, color, and spacing. The page title 'WAXING' is the primary focal point: largest text on the page, centered, letter‑spaced, and in a distinct blush color. A thin horizontal rule beneath reinforces the section break. The secondary level is the navigation bar at the very top, composed of evenly spaced uppercase links. Service names and prices form the tertiary level: medium‑size serif text arranged in rows, with consistent left‑right pairing (service → price). There are no heavy borders or backgrounds, so hierarchy depends on font size, weight, color, and alignment.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is very generous. Large vertical gaps separate the nav from the page title and the title from the service list, giving a light, airy feel. Horizontal spacing between service/price groups is also substantial, forming three visual columns with wide gutters. There are no enclosing boxes or shaded backgrounds; content appears to float on a clean white canvas.
unique_elements: Very sparse, menu‑like service list laid out in multiple wide columns, reminiscent of a restaurant menu.
Blush‑colored, all‑caps section title with subtle letter spacing as a key brand element.
Minimalist horizontal divider under the section heading with a very light, almost invisible color.
Fixed circular chat/help bubble icon in the bottom‑right corner with an outlined speech‑bubble symbol.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width central column, likely underpinned by a 12‑column grid with three equal content columns for the service list. Wide outer margins on both sides create a centered layout.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Centered, full‑width header/nav with a single centered content column beneath. Inside the content column, the service menu uses a multi‑column row pattern similar to a 3‑column menu grid.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top section: global navigation bar spanning the width, with links centered horizontally. Middle: large centered section heading ('WAXING') and a thin divider. Lower: a service menu block composed of rows, each containing multiple service‑price pairs laid out horizontally. There are no further sub‑sections shown in the screenshot.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall alignment is strongly centered for macro elements (nav group, page title, content block as a whole). Within the menu block, items are left‑aligned in each column, and prices are right next to their corresponding service names. Text baselines within each row are aligned horizontally, creating a clean grid.
responsive_hints: Multi‑column service layout suggests that on smaller screens, the service/price pairs would likely stack into one or two columns.
Central content column with generous side margins implies easy collapse to full‑width on mobile while retaining a centered alignment.
The fixed chat bubble in the lower right indicates awareness of persistent UI elements that will need repositioning or scaling on smaller viewports.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top‑to‑bottom flow: navigation bar at the top, followed by a large block of vertical whitespace, then a centered section heading with a subtle divider, and finally a multi‑row, three‑column service menu. Each row contains up to three service‑price pairs. Each pair is structured as: service name (left) followed by a small horizontal gap, then the price. The three pairs in a row are spaced evenly across the width, creating three informal columns with large gutters between them.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The upper half of the viewport is dominated by whitespace and the page title, giving the heading significant breathing room. The nav itself is visually light and occupies little vertical space, but due to spacing below it feels separated from main content. The lower half contains the service list, but even here line spacing is generous: each row has a notable vertical gap beneath it. Side margins are substantial, so the content column is narrower than the full viewport width, emphasizing a centered, editorial composition.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Low content density. The page displays relatively few items per vertical segment and relies on large margins and padding to create an airy, high‑end feel. There are no dense tables or tight stacks; each row of services has noticeable vertical separation. This aligns with a luxury beauty brand aimed at a calm, uncluttered experience.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye first hits the navigation at the very top (due to its position and uppercase text), then is drawn downward to the large blush 'WAXING' title in the center. From there, the thin line just below the title subtly signals a transition. The user’s gaze then drops into the menu area and begins scanning left to right across each row, zig‑zagging from the leftmost service/price pair to the rightmost, row by row. The persistent chat bubble at the bottom right forms a secondary focal point in the periphery.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit buttons are visible in the screenshot (aside from the chat bubble, which behaves like a button). Based on the overall aesthetic, primary buttons are likely simple rectangles or slightly rounded rectangles (2–4px radius), with a flat blush (#B89496) fill, white text, and no heavy shadows. Secondary buttons would likely be ghost buttons with a 1px blush or neutral gray border and transparent background. The chat bubble itself is a circular button ~44–52px in diameter, white fill, gray border, and a minimalist line icon in the center.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Primary actions likely use semantic <button> elements or <a> elements styled as buttons, often inside a flex container for alignment. The chat bubble is probably a fixed-position <button> or <a> with an <svg> icon nested inside, wrapped in a container div for positioning.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No traditional cards (boxed content) appear. Content sits directly on the white background without card borders. If cards are used elsewhere in the site, they are likely minimal: white background, 1px very light gray border (#E3DEDB), 4px corner radius at most, and subtle internal padding (16–24px) with no heavy shadow, matching the refined, flat aesthetic.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form elements appear here, but given the visual language, inputs are likely full‑width fields with thin 1px borders in a light gray (#E3DEDB) or soft blush, square or slightly rounded corners (2–4px), and generous vertical padding (10–14px). Labels likely sit above the input (not floating within), set in the same serif or a complementary sans‑serif, small but clear. Error states would use a subtle red border and small inline text, maintaining the minimalist look.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal top navigation with evenly spaced uppercase text links. Text appears in a neutral dark color (#111111 or similar) on a white background. The active page ('WAXING') is slightly emphasized—likely via either a subtle color shift (to blush) or a slightly heavier weight, without underline. There are no borders, background bars, or icons; the nav is extremely minimal, relying on typography and spacing. Hover states are likely a color change to the blush accent or an underline.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Service list behaves as a multi-column menu list. Each item is just text—service name followed by its price—with no bullet icons or separators. Items are medium‑large serif type, black/dark gray, with substantial horizontal and vertical spacing. There are no borders between rows; separation is achieved purely by whitespace. Each row contains up to three pairs, giving a clean, grid-like rhythm.

heading.visual_style: Elegant, high‑contrast serif with thin hairlines, moderate stroke contrast, and classic proportions. All‑caps with letter spacing for major headings gives a luxurious, editorial tone.

body.visual_style: Refined serif that remains readable at smaller sizes, likely the same family as headings but at lighter weight, or a complementary transitional serif. It appears neutral and classic rather than decorative.

font_sizes.h1: 36–42px (used for the 'WAXING' title, all caps with letter spacing)
font_sizes.h3: 20–22px (for subheadings or key service category titles, if used)

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical interactive components (buttons, inputs) would use 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding. The menu rows visually suggest around 24–32px vertical spacing between them, and large lateral gutters (~48–64px) between service/price columns.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (nav to title, title to content) are separated by generous margins in the 64–96px range, emphasizing the airy, editorial feel.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely have a hover effect where text color changes from dark to the blush accent, possibly with a subtle underline appearing.
The active nav page probably uses a persistent accent color or slightly heavier weight to indicate the current section without adding heavy UI chrome.
The chat bubble button in the bottom‑right likely has a hover state with a slightly darker border or soft drop shadow, and a scale‑up or subtle fade‑in animation when hovered or opened.
Transitions should be subtle and refined: 150–250ms ease‑in‑out for color changes and scale, avoiding overly bouncy or playful easing to maintain the luxe, calm tone.
Section headings or menu items could gently fade in or slide up on scroll, but with very light motion and low distance to preserve minimalism.
